title: Evidence and Features
subtitle: How raw telemetry is transformed into forecasting-ready evidence

nodes.label: Raw CPU
nodes.definition: Unprocessed CPU utilization samples collected from monitored systems.
nodes.whyItMatters: CPU behavior often reflects near-term saturation patterns.
nodes.safeSentence: Raw CPU is a primary signal for understanding workload stress.

nodes.label: Raw Memory
nodes.definition: Unprocessed memory usage samples collected from hosts and services.
nodes.whyItMatters: Memory pressure can create performance instability before CPU breaches.
nodes.safeSentence: Raw memory helps identify sustained pressure patterns.

nodes.label: Timestamp Normalization
nodes.definition: Converting event times to a consistent timezone and interval standard.
nodes.safeSentence: Normalized timestamps make telemetry comparable across sources.

nodes.label: Hourly Buckets
nodes.definition: Aggregating raw observations into hour-level summaries.
nodes.whyItMatters: Hourly buckets reduce noise while preserving operational rhythm.
nodes.safeSentence: Hourly aggregation helps reveal meaningful short-term patterns.

nodes.label: Daily Buckets
nodes.definition: Summarizing usage into daily trend points for medium-horizon review.
nodes.whyItMatters: Daily views improve interpretation of growth and seasonality.
nodes.safeSentence: Daily buckets support planning conversations and trend comparison.

nodes.label: Group by Application
nodes.definition: Organizing telemetry by application identity before feature extraction.
nodes.whyItMatters: Application-level grouping keeps accountability and context intact.
nodes.safeSentence: Grouping by application aligns evidence with service ownership.

nodes.label: Rolling Average
nodes.definition: A smoothed trend signal computed over a moving time window.
nodes.whyItMatters: Rolling averages show direction without overreacting to spikes.
nodes.safeSentence: Rolling average gives a stable signal for expected usage direction.

nodes.label: Rolling Peak
nodes.definition: The moving maximum over a recent interval to track stress ceilings.
nodes.whyItMatters: Peak behavior can trigger risk even when averages look stable.
nodes.safeSentence: Rolling peak captures worst-case pressure over recent history.

nodes.label: P95
nodes.definition: The 95th percentile usage level used as a robust upper-range indicator.
nodes.whyItMatters: P95 balances outlier resilience with practical risk sensitivity.
nodes.safeSentence: P95 is a practical high-usage signal for operational planning.

nodes.label: Growth Slope
nodes.definition: Estimated rate of usage increase over time from cleaned trend data.
nodes.whyItMatters: Growth rate determines how quickly headroom may be consumed.
nodes.safeSentence: Growth slope helps estimate when capacity pressure may escalate.

nodes.label: Data Quality Check
nodes.definition: Validation of completeness, consistency, and plausibility of telemetry.
nodes.whyItMatters: Poor data quality undermines confidence in any forecast output.
nodes.safeSentence: Quality checks ensure forecasting is based on trustworthy evidence.

nodes.label: Feature Table
nodes.definition: A structured dataset containing cleaned trend and risk features by interval.
nodes.whyItMatters: The feature table is the handoff artifact for forecast and risk evaluation.
nodes.safeSentence: Feature tables package evidence into reusable decision-support inputs.

paths.label: Raw Signals to Feature Table
paths.description: Transform raw CPU/memory telemetry into validated forecasting features.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how telemetry preparation produces a reliable feature table.
